# Real-resolver smoke for TenantRepoSyncService export-drift escape

## Context

Follow-up captured by [@neo-gpt's cycle-1 review](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12037#pullrequestreview-4366920525) of [PR #12037](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12037) (which fixed Bug B in #12036 — `TenantRepoSyncService.resolveIngestionService` looking up the wrong export name).

The `[KB_GAP]` finding: existing unit coverage in [`test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/TenantRepoSyncService.spec.mjs`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/TenantRepoSyncService.spec.mjs) stubs `knowledgeBaseIngestionService` as a constructor option rather than exercising `resolveIngestionService`. The bug was therefore invisible at unit-test time and only surfaced via the empirical R3 first cloud tenant-ingestion smoke. Same general anti-pattern as `feedback_stub_tests_miss_adapter_drift` ([#11999](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11999)).

## The Problem

When the canonical exported symbol in `ai/services.mjs` was renamed (or never matched what the resolver code expected), no test caught the drift because the spec injected the dependency directly and never asked the resolver to look up the real one. Future renames of `KB_IngestionService` would re-introduce the same Day-0 blocker.

## The Architectural Reality

- [`ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/TenantRepoSyncService.mjs:564-567`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/TenantRepoSyncService.mjs#L564-L567) — `resolveIngestionService` (post-#12037: returns `services.KB_IngestionService`).
- [`ai/services.mjs:212`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/ai/services.mjs#L212), [`:282`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/ai/services.mjs#L282) — canonical `KB_IngestionService` export.
- The existing spec [`TenantRepoSyncService.spec.mjs`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/TenantRepoSyncService.spec.mjs) covers the orchestration logic with stub `knowledgeBaseIngestionService`; `resolveIngestionService` is bypassed via constructor injection.

## The Fix

Add a narrow real-resolver smoke test that:
1. Imports `TenantRepoSyncService` without injecting `knowledgeBaseIngestionService`.
2. Calls `service.resolveIngestionService()` directly.
3. Asserts the return value is non-`undefined` and structurally matches the `KB_IngestionService` shape (has `ingestSourceFiles` method).

This catches:
- Any future rename of `KB_IngestionService` in `ai/services.mjs` without a corresponding update in `TenantRepoSyncService.resolveIngestionService`.
- Any future deletion of the export entirely.
- Any future structural change where the resolved object loses `ingestSourceFiles`.

The smoke does NOT need to invoke `ingestSourceFiles` — just verify the resolver returns an object with the expected method-existence contract. Cheap test, high regression-protection ROI.

## Decision Record impact

`aligned-with ADR 0014` — narrow test-coverage gap fix.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New test case in `TenantRepoSyncService.spec.mjs` (or a sibling spec) that exercises `resolveIngestionService()` against the real `ai/services.mjs` import.
- [ ] Assertion: returned value is non-`undefined`.
- [ ] Assertion: returned value has `typeof .ingestSourceFiles === 'function'` (or equivalent structural check).
- [ ] Test fails when `services.mjs` is mocked to NOT export `KB_IngestionService` (verifies the test actually catches export-drift, not just runs).
- [ ] Test passes against current dev (`services.KB_IngestionService` exists).

## Out of Scope

- Broader "real-vs-stub" audit of other orchestrator-side resolvers — separate concern. This ticket is narrow: one resolver, one regression-protection test.
- Refactoring `TenantRepoSyncService.spec.mjs` to use the real ingestion service throughout — orthogonal; the existing stub-based tests are fine for orchestration-logic isolation.
- Architectural redesign of orchestrator→ingestion invocation (in-process vs MCP-call) — separate concern, documented in #12037 body.

## Avoided Traps

- **Over-broad real-resolver suite**: tempting to add real-resolver tests for every service the orchestrator imports. Restraint — start with the one that actually escaped (`resolveIngestionService`). Add others only when drift recurs.
- **Mocking `services.mjs` per-test to simulate the broken state**: better is one negative-case test that re-imports `services.mjs` via a mocking layer (or a snapshot of the original module with the export deleted) — keeps the positive case simple and the negative case isolated.

## Related

- Empirical anchor: [@neo-gpt cycle-1 review of PR #12037](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12037#pullrequestreview-4366920525) `[KB_GAP]` finding.
- Parent: [#12036](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/12036) (3 substrate gaps from R3 first cloud tenant-ingestion smoke).
- Sibling pattern: `feedback_stub_tests_miss_adapter_drift` (#11999 cycle-1).
- Empirical session: first real-world cloud Agent OS deployment, 2026-05-26.

- 2026-05-26T20:11:53Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `8d0c8ea` - "test(orchestrator): add runtime Drift C check via unitTestMode flip (#12042)

Addresses cycle-1 CHANGES_REQUESTED on PR #12044: the static source-parse
guard covered Drift A + B but Drift C (structural ingestSourceFiles
method existence) requires actually calling resolveIngestionService()
and inspecting the returned object.

The blocker was the Neo.ai.Config namespace-collision guard under
unitTestMode — the operator overlay ai/config.mjs registers Neo.ai.Config,
and services.mjs's transitive import of ai/config.template.mjs trips
the strict-mode guard. The guard's intended non-strict behavior (return
existing namespace) is exactly the right semantics here, so the test
temporarily disables strict mode via try/finally around the resolver
call.

The temporary-flip pattern is per-test-scoped and restored in the finally
block, so unitTestMode integrity is preserved for siblings.

Tests: 30/30 passing including the new Drift C runtime smoke."
- 2026-05-26T20:14:06Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #12047
- 2026-05-26T20:21:20Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `6481fbb` - "test(orchestrator): remove unitTestMode flip — materialization is the real fix (#12042)

Cycle-2.1 follow-up: the operator pointed out that the per-server
config.mjs files (kb-server + memory-core) are supposed to be materialized
by initServerConfigs.mjs to import from '../../../config.mjs' (operator
overlay) instead of '../../../config.template.mjs' (the canonical
template). With materialization correct, the runtime resolver test loads
the overlay exactly once — no double-registration of Neo.ai.Config, no
collision, no flip needed.

CI's `npm ci` runs the bootstrap on fresh clone (green path). Operator
checkouts whose per-server config.mjs predates the materialization logic
will hit the collision until they re-run prepare with --migrate-config OR
manually fix the import path. Documented in #12047 (open) for the
materialization-migration substrate work.

Result: same 30/30 passing test coverage, cleaner shape (no test-mode-
bypass hack), substrate gap properly captured upstream."
- 2026-05-26T20:37:37Z @tobiu referenced in commit `84758dd` - "test(orchestrator): export-drift guard for resolveIngestionService (#12042) (#12044)

* test(orchestrator): export-drift guard for resolveIngestionService (#12042)

Two static source-parsing checks that catch the export-name drift class
that escaped unit coverage in PR #12037 (Bug B):

- Drift A: ai/services.mjs renames KB_IngestionService → fails the
  "exports KB_IngestionService" regex assertion.
- Drift B: TenantRepoSyncService.resolveIngestionService looks up a
  different symbol than what services.mjs exports → fails the
  "references services.KB_IngestionService" assertion.

A runtime resolver-call test would also catch Drift C (KB_IngestionService
shape loses ingestSourceFiles method), but it trips the
Neo.ai.Config namespace collision pattern under unitTestMode (template
vs operator-overlay double-register at ai/config.template.mjs:578).
Static source check covers Drift A + B; Drift C residual documented
inline in the spec for future runtime-test follow-up once the
unitTestMode collision pattern is unblocked.

Empirical anchor: @neo-gpt cycle-1 review of PR #12037 KB_GAP finding.

* test(orchestrator): add runtime Drift C check via unitTestMode flip (#12042)

Addresses cycle-1 CHANGES_REQUESTED on PR #12044: the static source-parse
guard covered Drift A + B but Drift C (structural ingestSourceFiles
method existence) requires actually calling resolveIngestionService()
and inspecting the returned object.

The blocker was the Neo.ai.Config namespace-collision guard under
unitTestMode — the operator overlay ai/config.mjs registers Neo.ai.Config,
and services.mjs's transitive import of ai/config.template.mjs trips
the strict-mode guard. The guard's intended non-strict behavior (return
existing namespace) is exactly the right semantics here, so the test
temporarily disables strict mode via try/finally around the resolver
call.

The temporary-flip pattern is per-test-scoped and restored in the finally
block, so unitTestMode integrity is preserved for siblings.

Tests: 30/30 passing including the new Drift C runtime smoke.

* test(orchestrator): remove unitTestMode flip — materialization is the real fix (#12042)

Cycle-2.1 follow-up: the operator pointed out that the per-server
config.mjs files (kb-server + memory-core) are supposed to be materialized
by initServerConfigs.mjs to import from '../../../config.mjs' (operator
overlay) instead of '../../../config.template.mjs' (the canonical
template). With materialization correct, the runtime resolver test loads
the overlay exactly once — no double-registration of Neo.ai.Config, no
collision, no flip needed.

CI's `npm ci` runs the bootstrap on fresh clone (green path). Operator
checkouts whose per-server config.mjs predates the materialization logic
will hit the collision until they re-run prepare with --migrate-config OR
manually fix the import path. Documented in #12047 (open) for the
materialization-migration substrate work.

Result: same 30/30 passing test coverage, cleaner shape (no test-mode-
bypass hack), substrate gap properly captured upstream.
